info.name: Path LMS Reporting API - Production Collection
info.description: This collection is a predefined list of api endpoints that will return data that matches the current reports in Path LMS. The main benefits of this API are: - You can run the report around multiple teams and also include or exclude as many fields as you need or don't need to make the data more useful for your data needs. - You can run mulitple reports against a team(s) in one call in order to more easily pull only data that brings you useful metrics. Below is an example of where to enter variables such as team ID's and run the query

graphql.query:
query events(
  $startDate: Date!,
  $endDate: Date!,
  $withArchived: Boolean!,
  $limit: Int,
  $offset: Int
  ) {
  account {
    id
    name
    events(withArchived: $withArchived, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      id
      status
      name
      archivedAt
      createdAt
      deletedAt
      startedOn
      endedOn
      presentationAmount(withArchived: $withArchived)
      presentations(withArchived: $withArchived) {
        id
        name
        parentEventSection
        userVisitsCount(
          startDate: $startDate
          endDate: $endDate
        )
        userVisits(
          startDate: $startDate
          endDate: $endDate
        ) {
          id
          firstName
          lastName
          email
        }
        playCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
        hoursWatched(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
        status
      }
      userVisitsCount(
        withArchived: $withArchived
        startDate: $startDate
        endDate: $endDate
      )
      userVisitsDetail(
        withArchived: $withArchived
        startDate: $startDate
        endDate: $endDate
      ) {
        email
        eventItemViews
      }
    }
  }
}

query ($startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $limit: Int, $offset: Int, $withArchived: Boolean) {
  account {
    assignmentsReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, limit: $limit, offset: $offset, withArchived: $withArchived) {
      id
      name
      courseAndSection
      createdAt
      totalSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      submissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate){
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
      }
      passedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      failedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      failedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      userVisitsCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      userVisits(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        uid
        visitedAt
      }
      credits
      status
    }
  }
}

query($startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $withArchived: Boolean!, $limit: Int!, $offset: Int!) {
  account {
    id,
    assessmentsReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, withArchived: $withArchived, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      id,
      name,
      parentCourseSection
      questionsCount
      failedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      averageScore(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      userVisits(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passingScore
      credits
      status
      assessmentQuestions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        question
        questionPool
        correctPercent(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
        submissionCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      }
      assessmentScores(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        id
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        uid
        score
        submittedOn
        spentTime
        status
      }
    }
  }
}

item.name: Example Custom Course User Visits Detail

graphql.query:
query($limit: Int, $offset: Int) {
  account {
    discounts(limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      id
      name
      status
      percentage
      amount
      times
      used
      details {
        order {
          id
          firstName
          lastName
          email
          date
          originalPrice
          discountAndCredits
          couponCode
          pricePaid
          refundTotal
          itemCount
          priceLabels
          notes
          paymentDetails          
        }
      }
      type
      updatedAt
      endDate
    }
  }
}

query salesByCategoryreport(
  $onlyHidden: Boolean
  $startDate: Date!
  $endDate: Date!
  $limit: Int
  $offset: Int
) {
  account {
    id
    name
    salesByCategoryReport(
      onlyHidden: $onlyHidden
      startDate: $startDate
      endDate: $endDate
      limit: $limit
      offset: $offset
    ) {
      name
      sales
      total
      revenue
      salesReport {
        coupon
        orderId
        discount
        itemType		
        itemName
        datePurchased
        originalPrice
        price
        firstName
        lastName
        email
      }
    }
  }
}

query($startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $withArchived: Boolean!, $limit: Int!, $offset: Int!) {
  account {
    id,
    salesByBundleReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, withArchived: $withArchived, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      orderNum,
      itemName,
      itemType,
      bundleName,
      status,
      presentedOn,
      datePurchased,
      originalPrice,
      discount,
      price,
      coupons,
      email,
      firstName,
      lastName,
      language,
      timeZone,
      stateCode,
      groups {
        name
      },
      physician,
      customAnswers {
        questionText,
        answerText
      },
      bundledSales(withArchived: $withArchived) {
        orderNum,
        itemName,
        itemType,
        bundleName,
        status,
        presentedOn,
        datePurchased,
        originalPrice,
        discount,
        price,
        coupons,
        email,
        firstName,
        lastName,language,
        timeZone,
        stateCode,
        groups {
          name
        },
        physician,
        customAnswers {
          questionText,
          answerText
        },
      }
    }
  }
}

request.description:
This endpoint will give you a list of team Ids and team names. Users can hit this end point with a valid authorization access token and find the team ids they would like to use in the reports.

```
id: A teams database record id
name: The name of a team as created in Path

```

This report will give you a high level overview of user information regarding work completed over the given start/end dates inputted.

By default all available user fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

```
id: A users database record id
email: A users email
firstName: A users first name
lastName: A users last name
registrationDate: The date a user created an account, accepted a registration link or was imported into Path
firstLogin: When the user first signed into their account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin: When the user last signed into their account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive: When the user was last seen. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
assessmentsPassedCount: The total number of assessments passed between the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
assessmentsFailedCount: The total number of assessments failed between the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
certificatesEarnedCount: The total number of certificates earned between the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
itemViewCount: This is a total number of items that a user has interacted with. (i.e. Taken a survey, assessment, watched a video, etc.)
prsentationsViewCount: The total number of presentatiosn a user has viewed beteween the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
language: The designated language for translations and text rendering in path for a user.
timeZone: The timezone a user is in and is selected on the user profile.
stateCode: The state a user designated in their profile as they are in.
countryCode: The country a user designated in their profile as they live in.
groups: This is a list of groups a user is designated in on an account.
customAnswers: If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.

```

This report will give you a an overview of course information regarding course activity over the given start/end dates inputted.

By default all available course fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Course Field Descriptions:

``` plaintext
name: Course Name
createdAt: The date that the course was created
userVisitsCount: This is the sum of all Item Visits by team users for the range between the start date and end date.
courseUserVisitDetails: This is user details specific to detailed infromation regarding user activity for the listed course.
    # Most user data here can be detailed from the user report field descriptions.
    # The fields listed below are user specific data for the course.
    courseItemViews: This is the total number of views a user had on the courses course items.
    courseProgressCompletion: This is the percentage of a course a user is completed to the current date the report is being ran on.
    
itemAmount: This is the total amount of Course Items on the Course listed.
status: This is the status of the course listed.
averageRating: This is the average rating users put on a course.
ratingsCount: This is the total number of ratings completed for a course.

```

This report shows Course Presentations progress/status including user details. This report is a high level report showing total number of course presentations for the date range between `startDate` and `endDate`

By default all available Course Presentations are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
    

Course Presentations Fields:

```
#Top level Course Presentations field information can be referenced in the field details of the course presentations report.
coursePresentations: This is the top level object that groups course presentation information together and details.
      name: Course Presentation Name
      parentCourseSection: The Courses parent course and section where applicable.
      totalVisits: Total course presentations visits by all users viewing the course presentations
      playCount: Total number of users that have played the presentation
      hoursWatched: Total hours watched by users on course presentation for a team
      credits: Credits associated to the course presentation earned when completed
      status: Current status of course presentation listed
    }

```

This report shows the total amount of certified credits for a user that have been earned during the date range given between `startDate` and `endDate`

By default all available certified credit fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Certified Credits Report:

```
firstName: The users first name
lastName: The users last name
email: The users email
username: The users username:
creditTypes: The grouped details of the credits listed with the earned credits totals
    name: The credit type name
    creditsCertifiedValues: The total number of credits earned by the user for the above credit type
certificatesCount: The total number certificates a user has earned against the detailed credit information above

```

This report shows credits completed by a user. This report is a high level report showing total number of credits completed by a user for the date range between `startDate` and `endDate`

By default all available credits completed fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Credits Completed Fields:

```
#Top level user field information can be referenced in the field details of the user report.
creditTypesForCompletedValues: This is the top level object that groups credit types that were successfully completed for a user during the specefied time frame.
    name: Credit Type Name listed on the account.
    creditsCompletedValues: This is the completed value for that user for that credit type.

```

item.name: Example Custom Course User Visits Detail

request.description:
This report shows the ability of merging multiple API reports together to get data that is useful for an account admin to make the report data work for what they want.

By default all available course fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

WC3 Custom Field Descriptions:

```
name: Course name
courseUserVisitsDetail
    # Most of this information can be found referenced in the users report and courses report field details
    
    courseItemViews: The total number of course items a user viewied for the listed course
    courseStartDate: This is the first time a user visited a course item and an action was saved
    courseUserCertificate: This is the first user certificate a user was awarded on a course and the date it was awarded.
    courseProgressCompletion: The percentage of a course that a user who started the course has completed.

```

This report shows certificate details for a team(s). This report is a high level certificate, totals count for awarded information. If you want to see the user awarded details and associated certificate item details you will want to run the `Certificates Report w/ Details`

By default all available certificate fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Certificates Report Fields:

```
name: This is the certificate name
courseAndSection: This is the associated course name and section name for the certificate.
awardCount: This is the total sum of awarded certificates over the range between startDate and endDate.
credits: If a certificate has credits associated to it, the type and the value will be displayed here.
createdAt: When the certificate was created.
certificateMetaData: Some certificates have metadata values and labels setup on them. That information will show up in these field.
itemCount: This is the total number of items on the associated certificate course.
status: Current certificate status.
templateName: This is the template name used for creating the certificate.

```

This report shows certificate details for a team(s) and their details breakdowns for `Award Count` and `ItemCount`.

By default all available certificate fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

  
  
  

```
#The top level fields listed can be found detailed out in the Certificates Report section above.

userCertificates:
    # user: These field details can be seen in the Users Report breakdown above and what the data represents.
items:
    id: This is the table id that the course item is referenced
    name: This is the course items detail name that shows when scrolling Course Items list
    type: This is the type of item listed (i.e. Pesentation, Link, Certificate, etc.)
    credits: This is credit details list if an item has credits associated.

```

graphql.query:
query ($teamIds: [Int!], $startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $limit: Int, $offset: Int, $withArchived: Boolean) {
  teams(teamIds: $teamIds) {
    assignmentsReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, limit: $limit, offset: $offset, withArchived: $withArchived) {
      id
      name
      courseAndSection
      createdAt
      totalSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      submissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate){
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
      }
      passedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      failedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      failedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      userVisitsCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      userVisits(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        uid
      }
      credits
      status
    }
  }
}

request.description:
This report shows the ability of merging multiple API reports together to get data that is useful for an account admin to make the report data work for what they want.

By default all available course fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

WC3 Custom Field Descriptions:

```
name: Course name
courseUserVisitsDetail
    # Most of this information can be found referenced in the users report and courses report field details
    
    courseItemViews: The total number of course items a user viewied for the listed course
    courseStartDate: This is the first time a user visited a course item and an action was saved
    courseUserCertificate: This is the first user certificate a user was awarded on a course and the date it was awarded.
    courseProgressCompletion: The percentage of a course that a user who started the course has completed.

```

This endpoint is designed to get a temporary token to validate requests by a clients application Id and application Secret.

This information will be provided by BlueSky.

After a successful request you will receive a token that you will then paste inside of the top level Authorization key.

1. Click on the Development folder.
2. Look for the key labeled `Authorization` and paste this token into the `Value` field. ** This will share the request across all endpoints.
    

**NOTE:** This token is only good for 12 hours. You will have to acquire a new token with this endpoint and update that Authorization Key/Value pair for future requests.

query.description: This is the startDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This is the endDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This parameter can be updated with whatever you want your CSV data to be seperated with. If this field is not present, it defaults to a comma.

request.description:
This endpoint is designed to get a report that is currently in our reporting API in a CSV format. We can utilize the power of GraphQL flexible API queries to get as much or as little data as needed, and render it in a CSV format for clients.

After a successful request you will receive a response in a csv format. If you specify a separator you can define what kind of separator you want your CSV data to be delimited with. **By default the separator is** **`,`**

response.name: CSV Request

query.description: This is the startDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This is the endDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This parameter can be updated with whatever you want your CSV data to be seperated with. If this field is not present, it defaults to a comma.

graphql.query:
query events(
  $startDate: Date!,
  $endDate: Date!,
  $withArchived: Boolean!,
  $limit: Int,
  $offset: Int
  ) {
  account {
    id
    name
    events(withArchived: $withArchived, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      id
      status
      name
      archivedAt
      createdAt
      deletedAt
      startedOn
      endedOn
      presentationAmount(withArchived: $withArchived)
      presentations(withArchived: $withArchived) {
        id
        name
        parentEventSection
        userVisitsCount(
          startDate: $startDate
          endDate: $endDate
        )
        userVisits(
          startDate: $startDate
          endDate: $endDate
        ) {
          id
          firstName
          lastName
          email
        }
        playCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
        hoursWatched(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
        status
      }
      userVisitsCount(
        withArchived: $withArchived
        startDate: $startDate
        endDate: $endDate
      )
      userVisitsDetail(
        withArchived: $withArchived
        startDate: $startDate
        endDate: $endDate
      ) {
        email
        eventItemViews
      }
    }
  }
}

query ($startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $limit: Int, $offset: Int, $withArchived: Boolean) {
  account {
    assignmentsReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, limit: $limit, offset: $offset, withArchived: $withArchived) {
      id
      name
      courseAndSection
      createdAt
      totalSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      submissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate){
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
      }
      passedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      failedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      failedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      userVisitsCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      userVisits(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        uid
        visitedAt
      }
      credits
      status
    }
  }
}

query($startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $withArchived: Boolean!, $limit: Int!, $offset: Int!) {
  account {
    id,
    assessmentsReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, withArchived: $withArchived, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      id,
      name,
      parentCourseSection
      questionsCount
      failedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      averageScore(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      userVisits(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passingScore
      credits
      status
      assessmentQuestions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        question
        questionPool
        correctPercent(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
        submissionCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      }
      assessmentScores(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        id
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        uid
        score
        submittedOn
        spentTime
        status
      }
    }
  }
}

item.name: Example Custom Course User Visits Detail

graphql.query:
query($limit: Int, $offset: Int) {
  account {
    discounts(limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      id
      name
      status
      percentage
      amount
      times
      used
      details {
        order {
          id
          firstName
          lastName
          email
          date
          originalPrice
          discountAndCredits
          couponCode
          pricePaid
          refundTotal
          itemCount
          priceLabels
          notes
          paymentDetails          
        }
      }
      type
      updatedAt
      endDate
    }
  }
}

query salesByCategoryreport(
  $onlyHidden: Boolean
  $startDate: Date!
  $endDate: Date!
  $limit: Int
  $offset: Int
) {
  account {
    id
    name
    salesByCategoryReport(
      onlyHidden: $onlyHidden
      startDate: $startDate
      endDate: $endDate
      limit: $limit
      offset: $offset
    ) {
      name
      sales
      total
      revenue
      salesReport {
        coupon
        orderId
        discount
        itemType		
        itemName
        datePurchased
        originalPrice
        price
        firstName
        lastName
        email
      }
    }
  }
}

query($startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $withArchived: Boolean!, $limit: Int!, $offset: Int!) {
  account {
    id,
    salesByBundleReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, withArchived: $withArchived, limit: $limit, offset: $offset) {
      orderNum,
      itemName,
      itemType,
      bundleName,
      status,
      presentedOn,
      datePurchased,
      originalPrice,
      discount,
      price,
      coupons,
      email,
      firstName,
      lastName,
      language,
      timeZone,
      stateCode,
      groups {
        name
      },
      physician,
      customAnswers {
        questionText,
        answerText
      },
      bundledSales(withArchived: $withArchived) {
        orderNum,
        itemName,
        itemType,
        bundleName,
        status,
        presentedOn,
        datePurchased,
        originalPrice,
        discount,
        price,
        coupons,
        email,
        firstName,
        lastName,language,
        timeZone,
        stateCode,
        groups {
          name
        },
        physician,
        customAnswers {
          questionText,
          answerText
        },
      }
    }
  }
}

request.description:
This endpoint will give you a list of team Ids and team names. Users can hit this end point with a valid authorization access token and find the team ids they would like to use in the reports.

```
id: A teams database record id
name: The name of a team as created in Path

```

This report will give you a high level overview of user information regarding work completed over the given start/end dates inputted.

By default all available user fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

```
id: A users database record id
email: A users email
firstName: A users first name
lastName: A users last name
registrationDate: The date a user created an account, accepted a registration link or was imported into Path
firstLogin: When the user first signed into their account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin: When the user last signed into their account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive: When the user was last seen. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
assessmentsPassedCount: The total number of assessments passed between the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
assessmentsFailedCount: The total number of assessments failed between the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
certificatesEarnedCount: The total number of certificates earned between the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
itemViewCount: This is a total number of items that a user has interacted with. (i.e. Taken a survey, assessment, watched a video, etc.)
prsentationsViewCount: The total number of presentatiosn a user has viewed beteween the date range of startDate and endDate for a user.
language: The designated language for translations and text rendering in path for a user.
timeZone: The timezone a user is in and is selected on the user profile.
stateCode: The state a user designated in their profile as they are in.
countryCode: The country a user designated in their profile as they live in.
groups: This is a list of groups a user is designated in on an account.
customAnswers: If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.

```

This report will give you a an overview of course information regarding course activity over the given start/end dates inputted.

By default all available course fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Course Field Descriptions:

``` plaintext
name: Course Name
createdAt: The date that the course was created
userVisitsCount: This is the sum of all Item Visits by team users for the range between the start date and end date.
courseUserVisitDetails: This is user details specific to detailed infromation regarding user activity for the listed course.
    # Most user data here can be detailed from the user report field descriptions.
    # The fields listed below are user specific data for the course.
    courseItemViews: This is the total number of views a user had on the courses course items.
    courseProgressCompletion: This is the percentage of a course a user is completed to the current date the report is being ran on.
    
itemAmount: This is the total amount of Course Items on the Course listed.
status: This is the status of the course listed.
averageRating: This is the average rating users put on a course.
ratingsCount: This is the total number of ratings completed for a course.

```

This report shows Course Presentations progress/status including user details. This report is a high level report showing total number of course presentations for the date range between `startDate` and `endDate`

By default all available Course Presentations are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
    

Course Presentations Fields:

```
#Top level Course Presentations field information can be referenced in the field details of the course presentations report.
coursePresentations: This is the top level object that groups course presentation information together and details.
      name: Course Presentation Name
      parentCourseSection: The Courses parent course and section where applicable.
      totalVisits: Total course presentations visits by all users viewing the course presentations
      playCount: Total number of users that have played the presentation
      hoursWatched: Total hours watched by users on course presentation for a team
      credits: Credits associated to the course presentation earned when completed
      status: Current status of course presentation listed
    }

```

This report shows the total amount of certified credits for a user that have been earned during the date range given between `startDate` and `endDate`

By default all available certified credit fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Certified Credits Report:

```
firstName: The users first name
lastName: The users last name
email: The users email
username: The users username:
creditTypes: The grouped details of the credits listed with the earned credits totals
    name: The credit type name
    creditsCertifiedValues: The total number of credits earned by the user for the above credit type
certificatesCount: The total number certificates a user has earned against the detailed credit information above

```

This report shows credits completed by a user. This report is a high level report showing total number of credits completed by a user for the date range between `startDate` and `endDate`

By default all available credits completed fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Credits Completed Fields:

```
#Top level user field information can be referenced in the field details of the user report.
creditTypesForCompletedValues: This is the top level object that groups credit types that were successfully completed for a user during the specefied time frame.
    name: Credit Type Name listed on the account.
    creditsCompletedValues: This is the completed value for that user for that credit type.

```

item.name: Example Custom Course User Visits Detail

request.description:
This report shows the ability of merging multiple API reports together to get data that is useful for an account admin to make the report data work for what they want.

By default all available course fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

WC3 Custom Field Descriptions:

```
name: Course name
courseUserVisitsDetail
    # Most of this information can be found referenced in the users report and courses report field details
    
    courseItemViews: The total number of course items a user viewied for the listed course
    courseStartDate: This is the first time a user visited a course item and an action was saved
    courseUserCertificate: This is the first user certificate a user was awarded on a course and the date it was awarded.
    courseProgressCompletion: The percentage of a course that a user who started the course has completed.

```

This report shows certificate details for a team(s). This report is a high level certificate, totals count for awarded information. If you want to see the user awarded details and associated certificate item details you will want to run the `Certificates Report w/ Details`

By default all available certificate fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

Certificates Report Fields:

```
name: This is the certificate name
courseAndSection: This is the associated course name and section name for the certificate.
awardCount: This is the total sum of awarded certificates over the range between startDate and endDate.
credits: If a certificate has credits associated to it, the type and the value will be displayed here.
createdAt: When the certificate was created.
certificateMetaData: Some certificates have metadata values and labels setup on them. That information will show up in these field.
itemCount: This is the total number of items on the associated certificate course.
status: Current certificate status.
templateName: This is the template name used for creating the certificate.

```

This report shows certificate details for a team(s) and their details breakdowns for `Award Count` and `ItemCount`.

By default all available certificate fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

  
  
  

```
#The top level fields listed can be found detailed out in the Certificates Report section above.

userCertificates:
    # user: These field details can be seen in the Users Report breakdown above and what the data represents.
items:
    id: This is the table id that the course item is referenced
    name: This is the course items detail name that shows when scrolling Course Items list
    type: This is the type of item listed (i.e. Pesentation, Link, Certificate, etc.)
    credits: This is credit details list if an item has credits associated.

```

graphql.query:
query ($teamIds: [Int!], $startDate: Date!, $endDate: Date!, $limit: Int, $offset: Int, $withArchived: Boolean) {
  teams(teamIds: $teamIds) {
    assignmentsReport(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate, limit: $limit, offset: $offset, withArchived: $withArchived) {
      id
      name
      courseAndSection
      createdAt
      totalSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      submissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate){
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
      }
      passedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      passedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      failedCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      failedSubmissions(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        submittedDate
        state
        gradedDate
        instructorEmail
      }
      userVisitsCount(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate)
      userVisits(startDate: $startDate, endDate: $endDate) {
        firstName
        lastName
        email
        uid
      }
      credits
      status
    }
  }
}

request.description:
This report shows the ability of merging multiple API reports together to get data that is useful for an account admin to make the report data work for what they want.

By default all available course fields are included in the section labeled `Fragment`. In order to exclude a field listed in your report, it is as simple as commenting out the field you do not need with a `#` at the beginning of the field name.

The variables listed in the request should be filled out with the desired information:

- `teamIds`: This is an array of teamIds that is desired to run the report. i.e. `[1,2,3]`
- `startDate`: This is the beginning date that you would like data to be pulled from and calculated.
- `endDate`: This is the end date taht you would like data to be pulled from and caclulated.
    

WC3 Custom Field Descriptions:

```
name: Course name
courseUserVisitsDetail
    # Most of this information can be found referenced in the users report and courses report field details
    
    courseItemViews: The total number of course items a user viewied for the listed course
    courseStartDate: This is the first time a user visited a course item and an action was saved
    courseUserCertificate: This is the first user certificate a user was awarded on a course and the date it was awarded.
    courseProgressCompletion: The percentage of a course that a user who started the course has completed.

```

This endpoint is designed to get a temporary token to validate requests by a clients application Id and application Secret.

This information will be provided by BlueSky.

After a successful request you will receive a token that you will then paste inside of the top level Authorization key.

1. Click on the Development folder.
2. Look for the key labeled `Authorization` and paste this token into the `Value` field. ** This will share the request across all endpoints.
    

**NOTE:** This token is only good for 12 hours. You will have to acquire a new token with this endpoint and update that Authorization Key/Value pair for future requests.

query.description: This is the startDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This is the endDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This parameter can be updated with whatever you want your CSV data to be seperated with. If this field is not present, it defaults to a comma.

request.description:
This endpoint is designed to get a report that is currently in our reporting API in a CSV format. We can utilize the power of GraphQL flexible API queries to get as much or as little data as needed, and render it in a CSV format for clients.

After a successful request you will receive a response in a csv format. If you specify a separator you can define what kind of separator you want your CSV data to be delimited with. **By default the separator is** **`,`**

response.name: CSV Request

query.description: This is the startDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This is the endDate you want data to be ran between when doing calculations
This parameter can be updated with whatever you want your CSV data to be seperated with. If this field is not present, it defaults to a comma.
